era: 1985-1992 full run. Proto-Grunge. E=7 -- heavy riff architecture, dark, direct, punk/metal interzone. U-District, Seattle.

theory_nodes.concept: Jack Endino: guitarist in the band AND producer of the scene. Production philosophy derived from performance experience inside Skin Yard.
theory_nodes.description: The producer was in the band. Minimum interference philosophy comes from knowing what it feels like to play heavy music.

theory_nodes.concept: Deep Six compilation (1986, C/Z): original document of the Seattle heavy underground. Daniel House founds label, curates six-band snapshot.
theory_nodes.description: Without Deep Six, the scene had no self-documentation at the moment of formation.

theory_nodes.concept: Matt Cameron origin: Skin Yard (1985-86) to Soundgarden (1986-97) to Pearl Jam (1998-present). Skin Yard is the beginning.
theory_nodes.description: The most consequential career origin in Seattle rock begins here.

theory_nodes.concept: Return cluster 297-300: Skin Yard, Treepeople, Love Battery, Pond. All returns. Same neighborhoods and labels. Engine consolidating history before five-sixths mark.

cross_references.relationship: First appearance. Endino as producer, Cameron origin, Deep Six, C/Z. Return focuses the full arc.
C/Z labelmate. Adjacent first appearances. Boise-Seattle pipeline.
C/Z cluster continues at 297-298. Two consecutive C/Z returns.
Fellow Deep Six band. Proto-grunge cluster. Sub Pop pivot.
Fellow Deep Six band. Andrew Wood. Mother Love Bone.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=7 = E=14. Riff architecture meets locked-talon aerial tumble. Sustained high-energy effort in different domains.
